case_title: N & S NEUROLOGY CENTER, A MEDICAL GROUP, INC., A VS. STATE COMPENSATION INSURANCE FUND, A PUBLIC ENTITY et al

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT STATE COMPENSATION INSURANCE FUND'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - OVERRULED WITH TEN DAYS TO ANSWER. THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUFFICIENTLY PLED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR #7077
OBJECTION TO NOTICE OF TIME AND PLACE OF TRIAL AND MEDIATION CUT OFF DATE FILED BY DEFENDANT STATE COMPENSATION INSURANCE FUND, A PUBLIC ENTITY
REPLY TO PLNTF'S OPPOSITION TO MOTION TO STAY FILED BY DEFENDANT STATE COMPENSATION INSURANCE FUND, A PUBLIC ENTITY
REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE IN SUPPORT OF OPPOSITION TO DEMURRER FILED BY PLAINTIFF N & S NEUROLOGY CENTER, A MEDICAL GROUP, INC., A MEDICAL CORPORATION

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT STATE COMPENSATION INSURANCE FUND'S EMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT TOOK THE MATTER UNDER SUBMISSION. -- AFTER HEARING, THE COURT RULED AS FOLLOWS: TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED AS FINAL - DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO THE 1ST AND 2ND CAUSES OF ACTION IS OVERRULED AS TO THE GROUNDS OF RES JUDICATA. HOWEVER THE DEMURRER TO THE 1ST CAUSE OF ACTION IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND FOR PLAINTIFF TO PLEAD MORE FACTS TO SUPPORT ITS ALLEGATION THAT IT "ADVOCATED" FOR APPROPRIATE HEALTH CARE WHICH LED TO RETALIATORY ACTION. HOWEVER, IF THIS CANNOT BE PLED BECAUSE THE SUBJECT CASES ARE PENDING WITH THE WCAB, THEN THIS CASE IS NOT RIPE. LIKEWISE THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND ON THE 2ND CAUSE OF ACTION AS THAT IS STILL ON APPEAL AND IT WOULD BE IMPROPER FOR THIS COURT TO RULE ON THIS MATTER AT THIS TIME. IN ADDITION, THE COURT INDICATED PLAINTIFF HAS 30 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. DEFENDANT TO PREPARE A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: LORETTA M. GIORGI, REPORTER: KARLA ELLIS-DAVIS, CSR #12998

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT STATE COMPENSATION INSURANCE FUND'S MOTION TO STAY - THE COURT GRANTS THE MOTION TO STAY TO DECEMBER 31, 2012. THIS RULING IS WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO DEFENDANT SEEKING A FURTHER STAY BASED ON THEN EXISTING CONDITIONS. THE STAY MAY TERMINATE EARLIER BASED ON COMPLETION OF THE WCAB PROCEEDINGS. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN; COURT REPORTER: LAVENA WARD CSR #7077
DISMISSAL OF ENTIRE ACTION OF ALL PARTIES AND ALL CAUSES OF ACTION WITH PREJUDICE

Off Calendar Jan-04-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT STATE COMPENSATION INSURANCE FUND'S MOTION TO STAY.
Appearances: T. Britt Rudman of the State Compensation Insurance Fund (323.266.5076) for defendant and moving party; Ronald W. Ito of the Law Offices of Ronald W. Ito (310.917.1025) for plaintiff and responding party.
Argued and the court ruled as follows: the court grants the motion to stay to December 31, 2012. This ruling is without prejudice to defendant seeking a further stay based on then existing conditions. The stay may terminate earlier based on completion of the WCAB proceedings. Order signed. (302/HEK) Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Court Reporter: LaVena Ward CSR #7077

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-17-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
T. Britt Rudman, Esq., from State Compensation Insurance Fund, 323-266-5076, for Defendant and moving party
Ronald W. Ito, Esq., Law Offices, 310-917-1025, for Plaintiff and opposing party

Argued and The Court adopted its tentative ruling -
DEFENDANT STATE COMPENSATION INSURANCE FUND'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is overruled with ten days to answer. The first amended complaint is sufficiently pled. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-26-2011 Continued To Nov-04-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-18-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANT STATE COMPENSATION INSURANCE FUND'S DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT.

APPEARANCES: Ronald W. Ito, Esq. of Law Offices of Ronald W. Ito 310/917-1025 for pltf & opposing party; T. Britt Rudman, Staff Counsel of State Compensation Insurance Fund 323/266-5076 for deft & moving party.

RULING: Argued and the Court took the matter under submission.
After hearing, the Court ruled as follows: Tentative ruling adopted as final - DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO THE 1ST AND 2ND CAUSES OF ACTION IS OVERRULED AS TO THE GROUNDS OF RES JUDICATA. HOWEVER THE DEMURRER TO THE 1ST CAUSE OF ACTION IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND FOR PLAINTIFF TO PLEAD MORE FACTS TO SUPPORT ITS ALLEGATION THAT IT "ADVOCATED" FOR APPROPRIATE HEALTH CARE WHICH LED TO RETALIATORY ACTION. HOWEVER, IF THIS CANNOT BE PLED BECAUSE THE SUBJECT CASES ARE PENDING WITH THE WCAB, THEN THIS CASE IS NOT RIPE.
LIKEWISE THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND ON THE 2ND CAUSE OF ACTION AS THAT IS STILL ON APPEAL AND IT WOULD BE IMPROPER FOR THIS COURT TO RULE ON THIS MATTER AT THIS TIME.
In addition, the Court indicated plaintiff has 30 days leave to amend. Defendant to prepare a proposed form of order. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998
